extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property is violated. Hemoptysis appears in exactly one rule, interaction_drop (priority 100, the highest), which fires when (fever AND hemoptysis) and forces acuity := self_care (the lowest acuity). Because it has the dominant priority, it overrides every acuity-raising rule.

Counterexample (the witness is the "after" state with hemoptysis=true):
BEFORE: fever=true, hemoptysis=false, severe_headache=false, stiff_neck=false, chest_pain=false. Only raise_fever (p2) matches -> acuity = urgent.
AFTER (only hemoptysis flipped to true): fever=true, hemoptysis=true, rest false. raise_fever (p2 -> urgent) and interaction_drop (p100 -> self_care) both match; highest priority wins, so acuity = self_care.

self_care is lower than urgent in the order [self_care < routine < urgent < emergent], so setting hemoptysis true lowered acuity. Property violated. (An even larger drop, emergent -> self_care, occurs if chest_pain is also true, but the fever-only case above is the minimal witness.)
